e2e_enforcement.blocking_reason: Close-debate QA gatekeeper role per commands/close.md Step 2 is a META verdict-deliberation role, not a user-facing-change verification role. This cycle's deliverables are .md/.json/.sh artifacts (verifier scripts, BA spec, dev report, close report) with NO web frontend, NO API endpoint, NO user-facing pipeline. Substantive E2E verification was already performed by the QA final-verification agent (qa-output-final-verification-20260519-211515.json, status=pass, 27 sub-verifiers + 35 pytest cases all PASS) prior to this close-debate dispatch. My role as close-debate gatekeeper is to audit verdict alignment and consult codex for adversarial review — neither requires browser/UI testing. Per agents/qa.md Step 10 skip-condition: 'change affects NOTHING the user can see or trigger: pure offline scripts, documentation-only changes, test-only changes, or hook/agent definition files'. This cycle's diff is entirely in that category (hooks/, agents/, commands/, .md docs, .json artifacts). Carve-out applies.
e2e_enforcement.qa_self_check: All 27 sub-verifiers and 35 pytest cases were executed by upstream QA final-verification agent and recorded in qa-output-final-verification-20260519-211515.json — that is this cycle's E2E equivalent for hook/script changes.
